name: Public Speaking Booster
description: 7 steps to confident speaking and presentations

activities.title: 30-Second Talk
activities.target: Speak on chosen topic for 30 seconds to 1-2 listeners
activities.script_parent: Pick favorite topic. Practice saying 3 things about it. Set timer for 30 sec. Start with just family!
activities.script_child: Tell us about your favorite [topic] for 30 seconds!
activities.success_criteria: Speaks for 25+ seconds on topic with outline or less 3/5 tries
activities.generalization: Family, then small groups, then class

activities.title: Posture and Breath
activities.target: Use power pose and deep breath before speaking
activities.script_parent: Before you talk: stand tall, shoulders back, take deep breath. Helps you feel strong! Practice together.
activities.script_child: Stand up tall and take a big breath. You've got this!
activities.success_criteria: Uses pose + breath before speaking 4/5 presentations

activities.title: Eye-Look Sweep
activities.target: Look at different sections of audience (not sustained eye contact)
activities.script_parent: Look at left side, then middle, then right. Or foreheads! Don't stare at one person. Practice with stuffed animals first.
activities.script_child: Look around at different people while you talk.
activities.success_criteria: Looks at 2+ different areas during 30-60 sec talk 3/5 times

activities.title: Cue Cards
activities.target: Create and use 3 cue cards with key words/images
activities.script_parent: Write/draw 1-3 words per card for each main point. Hold them! Glance down when needed. Practice with cards.
activities.script_child: Your cue cards will help you remember what to say!
activities.success_criteria: Creates cards with support and uses them during presentation 3/4 times

activities.title: Open/Close Lines
activities.target: Use practiced opening and closing statement
activities.script_parent: Start: 'Hi, I'm [name] and I'll talk about [topic].' End: 'Thank you for listening!' Practice until automatic.
activities.script_child: You'll start with your opening line and end with your closing line.

variants.open: Hi, I'm ___ and today I'll tell you about ___
Good morning. My name is ___ and my topic is ___
Hey everyone, I'm ___ and I want to share ___

activities.title: Practice With Timer
activities.target: Practice presentation 3 times, staying within time limit
activities.script_parent: Set timer for [required time]. Practice full talk. Check if under/over. Adjust. Do it 3 times before the real thing!
activities.script_child: Let's practice with the timer to make sure you're ready.
activities.success_criteria: Completes 3 practice runs within 10% of time limit
activities.mastery_rule: Success is being prepared (3 practices done)

activities.title: Celebrate the Attempt
activities.target: Identify one thing that went well after presenting
activities.script_parent: After presentation: 'What went well?' Help identify: loud voice, stayed on topic, eye contact, finished. Celebrate courage!
activities.script_child: You did it! What's one thing you did well?
activities.generalization: After every speaking experience, big or small

prompts: I did it even though nervous
